status_scope: Public maturity and gap status for CivitasX's own OrgAnchor adoption. This is not a new root authority statement and not a trust badge.

level_matrix.name: Minimal Verifiable Identity
level_matrix.summary: Root authority, signed official endpoint statement, public verify page, and CLI verification are present.

level_matrix.name: Public Verification Presence
level_matrix.summary: The Cloudflare Pages verification route is live and reachable, but the project still uses a shared provider subdomain and some domain controls require manual review.

level_matrix.name: Mirrored and Archived Identity
level_matrix.summary: GitHub and Cloudflare records exist, and a stronger carrier-ledger candidate passed locally, but the active public package does not yet include a signed lockfile, carrier receipts, IPFS CID, Arweave transaction, or Bitcoin-confirmed timestamp.

level_matrix.name: Disaster Recovery and Auxiliary Names
level_matrix.summary: A local clean-room recovery rehearsal passed from a local archive, but recovery from independently retrieved public artifacts has not yet been proven.

level_matrix.name: Governance and Migration Ready
level_matrix.summary: The current authority is a truthful 1-of-1 Temporary Founder Authority. Real threshold custody and signed migration require independent long-term custodians and are not complete.

live_package_gaps.summary: The active public verify package does not include a signed organchor.lock.json publication ledger.
The active public verify package does not include public IPFS, Arweave, OpenTimestamps, or equivalent carrier receipts.
No real IPFS CID or equivalent public content-addressed mirror is currently published for the active package.
A portable Arweave manual package candidate exists locally, but there is no public Arweave transaction.
Recovery has not yet been repeated using only externally retrieved public artifacts.
The project currently uses civitasx.pages.dev. A dedicated canonical domain and hardened naming strategy remain future work.
The current root authority is 1-of-1. Multi-custodian authority should wait for real independent custodians and must not be simulated.
No authority migration has happened, so no migration chain is included in the current package.
The current parent claims are supported by first-party evidence and reproducible public records, but not by independent S2/S3/S4 evidence.

recommended_next_actions.action: Keep this public adoption-gap status reachable from /verify/ and GitHub.
recommended_next_actions.reason: It makes current maturity legible without claiming a higher level.

recommended_next_actions.action: Decide whether to unfreeze and publish the already verified carrier-ledger candidate.
recommended_next_actions.reason: That would add a public signed ledger and timestamp material to the active package.

recommended_next_actions.action: Add at least one real independent carrier such as IPFS, Arweave, or another content-addressed or append-only archive.
recommended_next_actions.reason: Cloudflare and GitHub are useful but should not be the only long-term carriers.

recommended_next_actions.action: Repeat recovery from public artifacts only.
recommended_next_actions.reason: This tests whether later people can reconstruct the record without local files or the active Cloudflare account.

recommended_next_actions.action: Treat domain hardening and threshold custody as later structural work.
recommended_next_actions.reason: They are important, but should not be faked or rushed before the prerequisites exist.

non_claims: This status does not mean CivitasX is endorsed by OrgAnchor.
This status does not prove that CivitasX is correct.
This status does not prove decentralized governance.
This status does not make Cloudflare, GitHub, IPFS, Arweave, or any carrier the identity root.
This status does not grant authority to issue new official CivitasX statements without the current valid root authority or a later signed migration.
